name: Sinfonion
description: Harmonic CV processor. Three scale-aware quantizer channels, four-voice chord generator, eight-pattern arpeggiator and 16-song chord-progression sequencer share one key, scale and mode across eight 16-bit pitch outputs.

controls.name: ROOT
controls.description: Selects the root note of the current scale (C, C#, … B). Together with DEGREE defines the scale shown on the display.
controls.detail: 12 semitones · CV input sums · menu-assignable pot

controls.name: DEGREE
controls.description: Selects the degree / chord quality within the current mode. Combined with MODE picks one of 88 built-in scales.

controls.name: MODE
controls.description: Picks the musical mode. Chord-building modes (Chords, Chords2, Slashch) map buttons to jazz functions; scale modes (Major, Minor, Mel.Minor, Har.Minor, Har.Major, Db.H.Maj) map buttons to degrees.

controls.name: TRANS
controls.description: Global transposition in semitones. Applies to every pitch output.
controls.detail: ±12 semitones · noon = 0 · dedicated CV also available

controls.name: PRESET
controls.description: Selects one of 16 button presets in non-sequenced mode; becomes the song selector when SEQ is on.

controls.name: SLEW
controls.description: Master slew / portamento time. Per-section enable via SLEW buttons or IN 1/2.
controls.detail: Linear or exponential curve (menu) · CV attenuates when patched

controls.name: POT 1 / POT 2
controls.description: Two assignable knobs routable to 28 CV functions — e.g. Harmonic Shift, Chaotic Detune, Song select, per-channel Pre-Transpose.
controls.detail: Attenuates CV at the matching CV jack if both are assigned

controls.name: PITCH (Chords)
controls.description: Manual pitch control of the chord root. Sums with IN jack to set the lowest chord note.
controls.detail: 4 octaves · not saved in sequencer steps

controls.name: SPREAD
controls.description: Spaces chord voices apart. CCW = tight, CW distributes the four voices evenly across the selected range.

controls.name: PITCH (Arpeggio)
controls.description: Lowest note of the arpeggio pattern. Adds to IN jack and Pre-Trans CV.

controls.name: RANGE
controls.description: Range of the arpeggio in semitones. With range 0 the section acts as a clocked quantizer that grabs nearest allowed note.

inputs.name: CH 1 IN
inputs.description: Pitch input for Channel 1 quantizer. Routable via input-mixing matrix.

inputs.name: CH 1 TRIG
inputs.description: Sample-and-hold trigger. Rising edge quantizes the current IN voltage.

inputs.name: CH 1 A/B
inputs.description: Dedicated A/B switch. Above ~3V forces Channel 1 to use side B scale-note selection (only if A/B menu set to jack).

inputs.name: CH 2 IN
inputs.description: Pitch input for Channel 2 quantizer. Channel 2 exposes all 12 semitones via FILL1–FILL5.

inputs.name: CH 2 TRIG
inputs.description: S&H trigger for Channel 2.

inputs.name: CH 3 IN
inputs.description: Pitch input for Channel 3 quantizer. EQ-DIST button distributes allowed notes evenly across the input octave.

inputs.name: CH 3 TRIG
inputs.description: S&H trigger for Channel 3.

inputs.name: ROOT
inputs.description: CV for ROOT note. 1/12V per semitone, wraps around. Patch a pitch CV to track the incoming note's pitch class as the scale root.

inputs.name: TRANS
inputs.description: Global transposition (unquantized). 1V/Oct.

inputs.name: CV 1
inputs.description: General-purpose CV. Menu-assignable to one of 28 functions (Transpose, Chaotic Detune, Harmonic Shift, Preset, Slew Time, Pre-Trans, Pitch Bend, Arpeggio Clock/Randomize, Song, Song Part, Sequence Step, Voltmeter, …).

inputs.name: CV 2
inputs.description: General-purpose CV 2. Same assignment list as CV 1.

inputs.name: CV 3
inputs.description: General-purpose CV 3.

inputs.name: CV 4
inputs.description: General-purpose CV 4.

inputs.name: IN 1
inputs.description: Assignable gate input. 20 functions including Seq Song Reset / Part Reset / Step Forward-Backward, Slew-Enable per section, Quantize-Ch X, Chord Shuffle, Arpeggio Reset, Sync Slave.

inputs.name: IN 2
inputs.description: Second assignable gate input. Same function list as IN 1.

inputs.name: CHORDS IN
inputs.description: Pitch input for the chord section. Sums with PITCH knob to set chord root.

inputs.name: CHORDS TRIG
inputs.description: S&H trigger for the chord section — samples IN on rising edge.

inputs.name: ARP IN
inputs.description: Pitch input for the arpeggiator. Sets the lowest arpeggio note together with PITCH knob.

inputs.name: ARP CLK
inputs.description: Arpeggio clock. Normalled from main sequencer CLK; each pulse steps to the next arpeggio note.

inputs.name: CLK
inputs.description: Main sequencer clock. One pulse = one beat; chord change after step's beat count. Pre-divider (1–64) configurable per song part.

outputs.name: CH 1 OUT
outputs.description: Channel 1 quantized pitch output. Routable via output switching matrix.

outputs.name: OUT 1
outputs.description: Assignable gate / CV output. 14 functions: per-channel Autogate, Arpeggio Beat, Tuning Active, Sequencer Clock / Beat / Song-Step / Song-Reset, Sync Master.

outputs.name: OUT 2
outputs.description: Second assignable gate output. Same function list as OUT 1.

outputs.name: CHORDS OUT 1-4
outputs.description: Four independent chord voice pitches. OUT1 = first selected interval, OUT4 = fourth — stable mapping regardless of inversion or spread.

outputs.name: GATE 1 / GATE 2
outputs.description: Two manual gate jacks toggled by the GATES buttons. Gate states are saved per sequencer step.

behaviors.name: Three quantizer channels with different specialities
behaviors.trigger: Route a melody or random CV into CH N IN
behaviors.description: All three channels share the same scale / mode. Channel 1 has A/B-switch (auto-selects one of two note sets on alternating beats for 'root-on-1 walking bass' feel). Channel 2 FILL1–5 expose the five non-scale notes for chromatic access / major-minor flips. Channel 3 EQ-DIST equidistantly maps allowed notes across the input octave — critical when quantizing LFOs or random voltages.

behaviors.name: Four-voice chord generator
behaviors.trigger: Select any of ROOT, 3RD, 5TH, 7TH, 9TH, 11TH, 13TH in the CHORDS section; voices patched to OUT 1–4
behaviors.description: The first four lit interval buttons map to OUT1–4. Fewer than four lit → some voices double. More than four lit → only the first four are played. INV cycles through inversions (root / 1st / 2nd / 3rd). SPREAD knob opens voices across octaves. 98 possible voicings. Sus chords automatically remap 3RD button to 4th.

behaviors.name: Eight-pattern arpeggiator
behaviors.trigger: Patch clock to ARP CLK and voice to ARP OUT; select scale-note buttons
behaviors.description: Patterns: Up, Down, Up/Down, Down/Up, Baroque (2 forward, 1 back), Octaves (each note twice — once octaved up), Brownian (random by single step), Random. BEGIN forces reset to a specific interval on each chord change. Built-in clock divider/multiplier (1/4 to 4/1, incl. triplet 3/2) on the arp clock.

behaviors.name: Chord progression sequencer
behaviors.trigger: Press SEQ; clock into CLK jack
behaviors.description: 16 songs × 4 parts (A–D) × 32 steps. Each step stores root, degree, mode, beats (1–128), all 52 button states and all five octave switches. Clock divider 1–64 per part. ADD copies current step; DEL deletes; +/− BEATS tweak step length. Songs auto-save.

behaviors.name: Chord progression sequencer — live mode
behaviors.trigger: Press SYNC (Arpeggio) on the SONG page
behaviors.description: 'Buttons + Octaves are Live' makes button and octave changes immediate rather than overwriting the current step. The knob values still auto-save. Essential for improvising on top of a locked progression without scrambling stored steps.

behaviors.name: Freeze mode (hidden)
behaviors.description: Current chord and scale freeze. ROOT / DEGREE / MODE knobs and buttons only affect the display — no audible change. Lets a performer pre-dial the next section silently, then exit freeze to drop it live.

behaviors.name: Input & output matrices
behaviors.description: 4×4 input mixer (adds pitch from CH1/2/3/Arp IN jacks into any channel, with 2-octave subtract) and 4×4 output switch (each OUT jack reads exactly one quantizer). Unlocks voice swaps, unison plays and polyphonic routings impossible to build from external modules.

behaviors.name: Soft vs true bypass per channel
behaviors.trigger: 13TH in OCT page toggles CH 1/2/3 bypass state
behaviors.description: Normal → quantize · Soft Bypass (dim): raw pitch passes but transpose, chaotic detune, pitch bend and 'IN-follows-ROOT' still apply · Off (true bypass): 1:1 ADC→DAC pass-through with ~2mV precision — effectively a high-precision buffer.

behaviors.name: IN follows ROOT
behaviors.description: Automatically shifts the input pitch by the musical distance from C to the current root, so a bass sequence stays shaped around the new chord. Shifts of 7 semitones+ octave down instead of up.

behaviors.name: Chaotic Detune / Chaotic Note Shuffle
behaviors.description: Chaotic Detune: bipolar, detunes all eight pitch outputs independently up to 1 semitone — audio-rate modulation (1kHz update) produces disharmonic 'drunk-orchestra' motion. Chaotic Note Shuffle shuffles the 12 semitones within each octave globally for controlled dissonance.

behaviors.name: Harmonic Shift
behaviors.trigger: Assign CV 1–4 or POT 1/2 to Harmonic Shift
behaviors.description: Progressively strips harmonic complexity: at 0V all intervals allowed, 0.7V removes FILL notes, 1.4V 11th, 2.1V 13th, 2.9V 9th, 3.6V 7th, 4.3V leaves root+5th, 5.0V forces root only. Negative voltages remove in reverse order.

behaviors.name: Non-equal temperaments
behaviors.description: Switch globally between Equal, Just and Quarter-comma Meantone. Just retunes automatically when the root note changes — unique to Sinfonion and unavailable from any standalone quantizer.

behaviors.name: Tuning Mode
behaviors.description: Forces all eight outputs to a selectable octave C (1V steps from -2V/C0 to 8V/C8). Octave switches are honoured; CVs that modify pitch are suppressed. Use to calibrate external VCOs against Sinfonion's 16-bit DACs.

behaviors.name: Super-Sinfonion sync
behaviors.trigger: Set OUT 1 on master to 'Sync Master'; IN 1 on slave(s) to 'Sync Slave'; cable master OUT1 → slave IN1
behaviors.description: Serial link (115200 bps) sends ROOT / DEGREE / MODE / TRANS / Chaotic Detune and sequencer clock+reset over a single patch cable. Unlimited slaves via mult. Slave sequencer disables; master drives the chord/scale everyone plays in. Display shows 'Sync: alive'.

behaviors.name: Autogates and output triggers
behaviors.description: OUT 1/2 fires a 10ms trigger whenever a quantizer actually outputs a new semitone — great for re-triggering envelopes only on note changes. Also available: Arpeggio Beat, Sequencer Clock / Beat / Song-Step / Song-Reset.

patchIdeas.name: Minimum viable chord voice
patchIdeas.patch: Sequencer feeds CH 1 IN · CH 1 OUT to bass voice · CHORDS OUT 1-4 to four VCOs · MODE Chords · ROOT/DEGREE set chord · SPREAD opens voicing · one Sinfonion covers five-voice jazz harmony in one module.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sinfonion:
* MODE: Chords
* ROOT: C
* DEGREE: maj7
* Chords: ROOT 3RD 5TH 7TH lit
* SPREAD: noon

Bass VCO:

VCO 1:

VCO 2:

VCO 3:

VCO 4:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Sinfonion (CH 1 IN)
- Sinfonion (CH 1 OUT) p> Bass VCO (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Chord progression with clocked changes
patchIdeas.patch: Master clock to CLK · SEQ on · song 1A with 4 steps (Cmaj7 → Am7 → Dm7 → G7) · 4 beats each · CHORDS OUT 1-4 → four VCOs through one VCA pad · chord changes lock to clock.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sinfonion:
* SEQ: on
* Song 1A: Cmaj7 - Am7 - Dm7 - G7 (4 beats each)

Master Clock:

VCO 1:

VCO 2:

VCO 3:

VCO 4:

- Master Clock (Out) c> Sinfonion (CLK)
- Sinfonion (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Arpeggiated pad with pattern sweep
patchIdeas.patch: Clock into ARP CLK · arp OUT to VCO · select 1-3-5-7 · PATTERN Baroque · RANGE 2 octaves · PITCH sets lowest note · change BEGIN to 3rd for reset on chord change · deep jazzy motion that stays in-key under chord progression.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sinfonion:
* Arpeggio: ROOT 3RD 5TH 7TH lit
* PATTERN: Baroque
* RANGE: ~2 octaves
* BEGIN: 3rd

Clock:

VCO:

Envelope:

VCA:

- Clock (Out) c> Sinfonion (ARP CLK)
- Clock (Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Sinfonion (ARP OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Walking bass with root on 1 and 3
patchIdeas.patch: Random CV to CH 1 IN · CH 1 side A: 5TH 7TH 9TH · side B: ROOT · Menu → Ch 1 A/B 'Each Odd Beat' · sequencer on with 4-beat bar · bass plays root on beats 1 & 3, scale notes on 2 & 4 — walking-bass feel locked to chord changes.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sinfonion:
* SEQ: on
* CH 1 A side: 5TH 7TH 9TH
* CH 1 B side: ROOT
* Menu: Ch 1 A/B = Each Odd Beat

Random CV:

Clock:

Bass VCO:

- Clock (Out) c> Sinfonion (CLK)
- Random CV (Out) >> Sinfonion (CH 1 IN)
- Sinfonion (CH 1 OUT) p> Bass VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Equidistant quantizer for LFO melodies
patchIdeas.patch: Slow LFO into CH 3 IN · CH 3 ROOT 5TH 9TH lit · press EQ-DIST · each of the three notes gets equal time across the LFO's octave — fixes the 'root-is-always-playing' bias of classical quantizers.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sinfonion:
* CH 3: ROOT 5TH 9TH lit
* CH 3 EQ-DIST: on

LFO:

VCO:

- LFO (Out) >> Sinfonion (CH 3 IN)
- Sinfonion (CH 3 OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Harmonic shift drop
patchIdeas.patch: Assign POT 1 → Harmonic Shift · chord with 3rd, 7th, 9th, 11th lit · turn POT 1 CW during a drop — harmonic complexity strips back to root+5th then just root, useful for dramatic tension → release transitions.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sinfonion:
* Assign POT 1: Harmonic Shift
* Chords: ROOT 3RD 5TH 7TH 9TH 11TH lit

VCO 1:

VCO 2:

VCO 3:

VCO 4:

- Sinfonion (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Autogate re-triggers envelope on note changes
patchIdeas.patch: Menu → OUT 1 = Chan 2 Autogate · sequencer into CH 2 IN · VCO hears CH 2 OUT · envelope triggered by OUT 1 · envelope only re-fires when Channel 2 quantizes to a new semitone — repeated identical notes don't re-articulate.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sinfonion:
* Menu: OUT 1 = Chan 2 Autogate
* CH 2: ROOT 3RD 5TH 7TH 9TH lit

Sequencer:

VCO:

Envelope:

VCA:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Sinfonion (CH 2 IN)
- Sinfonion (CH 2 OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion (OUT 1) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Input matrix unison swap
patchIdeas.patch: Send CH 1 IN to Channel 1 AND Channel 2 · CH 2 OUT plays Channel 1's input through Channel 2's button preset · momentarily different voicing on voice 2 while voice 1 continues · layered polyphony from one input stream.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sinfonion:
* Input Matrix: CH 1 IN → Ch1 + Ch2
* CH 1: ROOT 5TH
* CH 2: ROOT 3RD 7TH

Sequencer:

VCO 1:

VCO 2:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Sinfonion (CH 1 IN)
- Sinfonion (CH 1 OUT) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion (CH 2 OUT) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Super-Sinfonion jam
patchIdeas.patch: Master Sinfonion runs song progression · OUT 1 = Sync Master to slave's IN 1 (Sync Slave) · slave chord section plays additional voices locked to master's key · eight more voices join the band with one cable.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sinfonion Master:
* Menu: OUT 1 = Sync Master
* SEQ: on

Sinfonion Slave:
* Menu: IN 1 = Sync Slave
* Chords: ROOT 3RD 5TH 9TH lit

- Sinfonion Master (OUT 1) >> Sinfonion Slave (IN 1)
- Sinfonion Slave (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 5 (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion Slave (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 6 (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion Slave (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 7 (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion Slave (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 8 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Just-intoned pad drift
patchIdeas.patch: Menu → Intonation: Just · sustained chord · ROOT CV tied to song progression — every chord change auto-retunes all twelve semitones to that root's pure intervals. Beats between 3rds / 5ths vanish and reappear as harmony shifts.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sinfonion:
* Menu: Intonation = Just
* SEQ: on (song: I-vi-IV-V)
* Chords: ROOT 3RD 5TH 7TH lit

Master Clock:

VCO 1:

VCO 2:

VCO 3:

VCO 4:

- Master Clock (Out) c> Sinfonion (CLK)
- Sinfonion (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- Sinfonion (CHORDS OUT 1-4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)
